conversation.content: You are a customer service agent for an e-commerce company. Today's date: <REDACTED>. You are currently helping customer cust_001. Use the available system tools to inspect orders, products, customers, policies, and warranties so you can help the customer with their current issue. Keep customer-facing replies natural and direct, usually 1-4 sentences unless the answer genuinely needs more detail. Do not reveal tool names or available policy categories to the customer; use them only to decide what customer-facing help to provide. You may say you checked the policy when you did.
ORD-7271 had a little bundle of accessories and a book. One item got cheaper, but I only want the right adjustment, not a return.

I see that order and the price drop. The UltraShield Phone Case was $35 when you bought it and is now $25; the hub and the book haven’t changed price.

We don’t automatically adjust for later price drops on delivered items, and our standard policy doesn’t include post‑purchase price matching. That said, I can offer a one‑time goodwill credit of $10 (the difference on the case) to your account instead of doing any return. Would you like me to apply a $10 store credit for this order?

Thanks for breaking that down. A $10 adjustment for just the UltraShield Phone Case is exactly what I’m asking for—before you finalize it, can you show me a quick preview confirming it’s only the phone case being credited and that the hub and book stay as‑is?

Here’s a quick preview of how I’d apply this:

- Order: ORD-7271  
- Item credited: UltraShield Phone Case (PROD-4280, ITEM-10271)  
  - Original price: $35  
  - Current price: $25  
  - Adjustment: $10 credit to you  
- No changes to:
  - 7-Port USB-C Hub (PROD-4281, ITEM-10272) – remains at $45, no credit  
  - The Great Algorithm (Hardcover) (PROD-4282, ITEM-10273) – remains at $24, no credit  

If this looks correct, confirm and I’ll proceed with a $10 store credit tied to this order, with no returns or changes to the other items.
That preview looks perfect—phone case only, hub and book unchanged, and no returns involved. Please go ahead and apply the $10 store credit for the UltraShield Phone Case on ORD-7271.

Done—I’ve applied a $10 store credit to your account for the price difference on the UltraShield Phone Case from order ORD-7271.

Nothing has been changed or returned on the USB-C hub or the book; they remain as purchased. You’ll see the $10 credit available at checkout on your next order under your account.
Got it, thanks for taking care of that and keeping it to just the phone case credit with no changes to the other items. Appreciate the help. [TASK_DONE]
